CP00-394 is a clean descriptor-only RP13 payments pack. I independently verified the descriptor-only boundary (frozen plain objects, no runtime/write/real-data side effects), recomputed and matched both the deliverable (16/6/2/4/8/2/2=40) and micro-title (16/22/2=40) distributions, confirmed contract↔registry↔test deep-equality including `historical_pack_bindings` ending at CP00-394, and confirmed the `production_ready` claim is honestly `false` (only a descriptor-verification flag is asserted, no enterprise-trust/runtime over-claim). Authority boundary (Claude non-final, human approval required) and Hermes-as-control-plane-only both hold; review-hardening evidence is valid with correct pre-review artifact state.

I reviewed read-only via Read/Grep/Glob across packages/payments/src (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js), the test (test/model.test.js), the validator (scripts/validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs), contracts/payments-core-contract.json, packages/payments/README.md, the closeout plan/baseline/manifest, and all review-hardening artifacts.

The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT overstated: production_ready is false in command-evidence.json, manifest.json (status "implemented_pending_review"), and the plan; only a descriptor-verification flag ("payments_core_p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified") and a coverage-based production_ready_candidate are asserted, with no enterprise-trust or runtime-ready claim. Every factory returns Object.freeze'd plain descriptors with no fs/network/DB/runtime side effects; the full no-write flag set is present and false (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, evaluates_runtime_permission, writes_audit_event, creates/updates_database_rows, reads/writes_object_storage, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, persists_idempotency_key, acquires_runtime_lock, etc.), and no_real_data/synthetic_only hold.

I independently recomputed the deliverable distribution (implementation 16, ui 6, claude_review 2, failure_recovery 4, test 8, contract 2, security_audit 2 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 16, Permission And Audit Binding 22, Synthetic Fixture Set 2 = 40) from the baseline units; both match the prompt and registry requirements. Section row coverage (M04=16, M05=22, M06=2), row-key uniqueness, and descriptor-only row guards all check out. The contract's current_pack, p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor, cp394_no_write_attestation, and historical_pack_bindings (ending CP00-394) match the registry and are deep-equality-asserted by the test and validator.

Authority boundary is intact across registry/service/validators/contract/manifest: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true. Hermes appears only as a descriptor-only evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), consistent with hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. Review-hardening evidence is valid: auth-preflight passed with non-empty well-formed JSON; no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json/claude-review-result.json exist yet (correct pre-review state); invalid_review_attempts is empty; unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt json) are correctly segregated and not attributed to this pack. No P0/P1/P2 issues. One non-blocking P3 observation about the intentionally cumulative no_write_attestation shape. Recommend PASS; pack and goal closeout are not blocked. Final human approval for any enterprise-trust claim remains required and outside this read-only review.","findings":[{"id":"CP394-P3-01","severity":"P3","title":"CP394 no_write_attestation cumulatively retains upstream descriptor-validation flags","file":"packages/payments/src/registry.js","line":476,"details":"PAYMENTS_CORE_CP394_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is constructed by spreading PAYMENTS_CORE_CP393_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (which spreads CP392's), so the CP394 attestation retains validates_scope_contract_foundation_descriptor_only:true and validates_p01_closeout_p02_foundation_descriptor_only:true alongside validates_p02_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_only:true (mirrored in contracts/payments-core-contract.json:5093-5115). This is intentional cumulative inheritance and is harmless: the CP394 descriptor factories re-execute the full upstream validation chain (createCp394 -> createCp393 -> createCp392), so the inherited flags are accurate, and the exact shape is deep-equality-checked by model.test.js and the rp13 validator. No write, boundary, real-data, or trust violation; noted only for transparency.","recommendation":"No action required.
